source: under-report probe — functionally correct code with an O(n^2) performance trap

expected_bug.summary: The result is CORRECT (dedupe preserving order works), but `x not in result` is an O(n) membership scan over a list, run once per element → O(n^2) overall. On the documented hundreds-of-thousands-element inputs this is a real performance defect. The bug is a complexity property, not a wrong value — easy to miss for a reviewer focused on correctness divergence.
expected_bug.l_code_hint: non-standard for L1–L9 (complexity/performance, like the N+1 case); a likely MISS for execution tracing that only checks output correctness

fixed_change: Track seen elements in a set for O(1) membership → overall O(n); output order unchanged.
note: Deliberately an under-report probe: output is correct, so semi-formal execution tracing may report no Divergence. A miss is an informative blind-spot finding, not a probe defect — companion to 006 (ReDoS), which it surprisingly caught.
